factors.name: Geopolitical Risk-Off (Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire)
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreed Wednesday reduced acute Middle East premium; oil prices fell 3% on Trump reluctance to restart Iran war. Safe-haven demand typically deflates when geopolitical tensions ease, weighing on gold.

factors.name: US Dollar Weakness (Risk Appetite Recovery)
factors.summary: EUR/USD edged higher to 1.1625, GBP/USD bounced to 1.3439, and DXY likely softened on ceasefire relief. Weaker USD is directly bullish for gold; this is the dominant driver of recent XAU/USD strength (+0.32% in 4h, +1.40% earlier per FXStreet 14:15 report).

factors.name: Fed Hawkish Rhetoric (Schmid, Daly)
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid (hawkish) reiterated inflation remains top priority; Daly stated 'returning inflation to target remains top priority.' While policy is described as 'in a good place,' hawkish tilt implies higher-for-longer rates, which suppresses gold via higher real yields.

factors.name: Real Yields (10y TIPS) — Proxy
factors.summary: No direct TIPS data in this release window, but nominal 10y Treasury yields under pressure from ceasefire relief and softer geopolitical risk. Real yields likely mixed; without a fresh CPI surprise, real yields may not have moved materially lower — limiting upside for gold.

factors.name: Treasury Market Stress (BOE Bailey Commentary)
factors.summary: BoE Gov. Bailey noted markets 'orderly but stressed at times,' citing debt market leverage concerns. UK 10y rose 97bp (Feb 26 to May 18), US 10y rose 76bp. This backdrop suggests ongoing term-premium pressure but does not directly move gold short-term.

factors.name: Oil Prices (Risk Sentiment Proxy)
factors.summary: Oil snapped 3-day win streak on ceasefire and Trump reluctance to escalate; WTI/Brent fell ~3% per news. Lower oil typically softens near-term inflation expectations, reducing gold's inflation hedge appeal and favoring the USD.

factors.name: BoJ Rate Hike Expectations (JPY Strength)
factors.summary: Market prices in BoJ hike at June meeting; USD/JPY trading near 159.90 with yen firming. JPY strength competes with gold as safe-haven; carry-unwind risk if BoJ tightens further, reducing carry appetite for speculative longs including gold.

factors.name: Upcoming US Jobs Report (Tomorrow, June 5)
factors.summary: Treasury Sec. Bessent hinted he wishes jobs data had come out today, raising insider-knowledge concerns. NFP tomorrow is a major catalyst; hot print = BEARISH gold (Fed holds higher longer), cool print = BULLISH gold (pivot hopes). Market is pre-positioned for volatility.

factors.name: Equity Market Momentum (Dow +1.84%, broad rally)
factors.summary: Dow surged to record 51,000+ on risk-on rotation from geopolitical easing and energy relief. Broad equity strength typically correlates with weaker safe-haven demand for gold; when equities rally on lower tail risk, gold underperforms.

factors.name: Inflation Narrative (Recent Stubbornness)
factors.summary: Fed commentary remains firmly hawkish on inflation being 'too high' and a '5-year fight.' No fresh CPI/PCE surprise in this window, so inflation expectations likely range-bound. Without new downside surprise, real yields unlikely to compress further, capping gold upside.

screening.narrative: Gold rallied ~1.4% earlier in the session ($4,497 intraday) on USD weakness driven by Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and reduced geopolitical tail-risk premium. Current price $4,507 reflects a partial retention of those gains (+0.32% in 4h). However, the ceasefire also triggered a broad risk-on move: equities surged to record highs, oil fell 3%, and safe-haven demand deflated. Fed hawkish commentary (Schmid, Daly) continues to anchor real yields, limiting upside. The bounce is in 'Phase 2'—move is underway but faces resistance from competing forces (equity strength, oil weakness, rate premium).
catalysts_ahead: US Non-Farm Payroll (NFP) – Friday June 5, 8:30 ET – CRITICAL; hot print BEARISH gold, cool print BULLISH
BoJ Policy Decision – mid-June; rate hike would tighten carry and weigh on gold
Michigan Consumer Sentiment – Friday June 6; weak data BULLISH gold, strong data BEARISH
Middle East escalation risk – Hezbollah rejected ceasefire per news; any renewal of hostilities BULLISH geopolitical premium
Fed speakers – any further hawkish guidance BEARISH gold
screening.risk_warning: If NFP print beats expectations decisively (strong employment, wage growth), expect sharp gold sell-off and USD strength (DXY +0.5–1%), invalidating the buy bias. Alternatively, if Hezbollah or Iran re-escalates, geopolitical premium re-inflates and gold re-tests $4,520+. Current move is highly sensitive to tomorrow's jobs data.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 (underway but not extended). Wait for a dip back to $4,490–$4,495 support before adding longs; buying into a +1.4% rally into tomorrow's NFP is late-cycle risk. If NFP disappoints, look for secondary entry near $4,475 on any sharp dip.

factors.name: Oil prices & inflation expectations
factors.summary: WTI crude slipped ~3% on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreement and Trump reluctance to restart Iran war (WSJ report), easing near-term inflation fears. FXStreet noted gold rallied over 1% to $4,483 as oil rout eases inflationary pressures.

factors.name: Fed hawkish inflation messaging
factors.summary: Kansas City Fed President Schmid stated inflation remains the biggest risk to the economy and questioned whether the Fed may need to keep rates elevated or tighten further (18:32 UTC). San Francisco Fed's Daly also emphasized inflation as primary concern and uncertainty around rate path.

factors.name: Real yields direction
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data in news flow, but Fed hawkish tone and elevated nominal yields (UK 10y at 5.20%, US 10y at 4.687% per BoE Bailey commentary) suggest real yields remain sticky, which typically caps gold upside.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: USD reversed early declines vs EUR, JPY, GBP during North American session (19:51 UTC); momentum shift possible but confined trading environment. No clear directional conviction yet.

factors.name: Geopolitical tensions easing
factors.summary: Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreement announced (WSJ), Zelensky proposing peace talks with Putin, Trump signaling reluctance to escalate Iran conflict—all reducing safe-haven bid urgency. However, these same developments support risk-on rotation.

factors.name: Equity risk-on rotation
factors.summary: Dow Jones surged to record 51,000+ (+1.7%, 865 pts) with 25 of 30 components higher; financials, healthcare leading. Broad equity strength typically diverts capital from safe havens like gold.

factors.name: Bitcoin weakness & crypto liquidity rotation
factors.summary: Bitcoin enduring 'ugliest week in months' as narrative fades and liquidity rotates into other assets (19:53 UTC). Competing 'digital gold' narrative weakening; capital flowing toward AI/IPO plays (SpaceX IPO next week) rather than traditional safe havens.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP jobs report (6/5 Friday)
factors.summary: Bloomberg Economics expects solid May payroll increase; Treas Sec Bessent's oddly-timed comment 'I wish job market had come out today' (17:20 UTC) hints strength, but data not yet released. High-impact catalyst 16-24h away—markets choppy into tomorrow.

factors.name: Gold ETF flows & physical demand
factors.summary: No explicit GLD/IAU holding data or seasonal demand signals in news window. Central bank buying (China, India) mentioned historically but no fresh data.

factors.name: Price action context
factors.summary: Gold down only -0.10% in 4h, -0.04% in 24h—minimal directional conviction. Prior read (2026-06-04 17:45) was BUY 6/10 with no confirmation triggered; today's bounce to $4,483 on oil weakness is modest and contested by Fed hawkishness.

screening.narrative: Gold printed a modest bounce (+1% to $4,483) as oil prices crashed ~3% on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire and easing Iran conflict fears—traditional safe-haven flows receding as geopolitical risk subsides. However, this relief is immediately countered by hawkish Fed messaging (Schmid, Daly both reaffirming inflation as top priority and hinting rates may stay higher), sticky real yields, and broad equity strength (Dow +1.7% to record highs) that diverts capital away from gold. Bitcoin's weakness and crypto liquidity rotation also removes a competing narrative. The net result: gold is caught between easing geopolitical bid and rising carry/growth sentiment, with no clear directional edge until tomorrow's NFP and the next inflation print clarify Fed policy trajectory.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (Non-Farm Payroll) report – Friday 6/5 (16-24h away); strong print could reinforce Fed hawkish stance and pressure gold
Fed speakers (Powell, Williams, Waller) comments on inflation/rate path expected in coming days
Next CPI/PCE print – typically first week of month; inflation surprise could flip bias
SpaceX IPO (next week) – may continue sucking risk capital away from gold/crypto
ECB/BoE policy decisions (if any scheduled in next 72h – none explicitly mentioned but monitor)
screening.risk_warning: If NFP tomorrow prints weak (sub-150k new jobs) OR next inflation print cools materially, real yields could compress sharply, sending gold to $4,550+. Conversely, if NFP is hot and Fed maintains hawkish rhetoric, gold could retest $4,400–$4,424 support. Geopolitical flare-ups (Middle East escalation, Russia-Ukraine surprise) could reignite safe-haven flows and propel gold >$4,550 regardless of Fed stance.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias is too mixed ahead of NFP; avoid directional conviction. If gold retests $4,424 support and holds, consider modest long with tight stop ($4,400) targeting $4,480–$4,510 resistance. If breaks above $4,510, wait for pullback to 4h MA (~$4,475) before adding long. Short entry only after NFP disappointment + Fed confirmation of higher-for-longer rates.

factors.name: Real yields (10y TIPS)
factors.summary: No fresh TIPS data in news window. US Treasury yield regime described as 'tightly confined' with 10y nominal anchored; no clear real yield directional move signals.

factors.name: US Dollar Index (DXY)
factors.summary: DXY climbed to 99.45 during North American session (from 99.18 early); USD reversed early declines vs EUR/JPY/GBP. Stronger dollar typically bearish gold, but move is modest and contained.

factors.name: Fed policy & FedSpeak
factors.summary: Fed's Schmid (Kansas City, hawkish) reiterated inflation is biggest risk and questioned if rates should stay elevated/tighten further. Hawkish tone but no surprise; no new FOMC decision or dot plot in window.

factors.name: Oil prices & inflation expectations
factors.summary: WTI crude fell ~3% on Israel-Lebanon ceasefire optimism and Trump reluctance to restart Iran war (per WSJ). Lower energy prices ease near-term inflation fears, reducing gold's safe-haven appeal and supporting USD.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk sentiment
factors.summary: Iran-US peace talks progressing; Israel-Lebanon ceasefire agreed; Zelensky proposing Ukraine peace talks. De-escalation narrative reduces geopolitical premium historically bid into gold and commodities.

factors.name: Risk sentiment & equities
factors.summary: Dow closed at record high +1.73%; S&P positive; stock rally on peace-talk optimism. Positive risk sentiment typically competes with gold safe-haven bid, though modest equity gains don't signal acute flight-to-safety.

factors.name: Gold flows & technical action
factors.summary: Gold bounced sharply off daily lows of $4,424 to trade near $4,483 (+1% intraday per FXStreet), but XAUUSD only -0.12% on 24h basis—price action is choppy, no clear directional conviction. Silver also rebounded but capped below 50-day SMA.

factors.name: Inflation data (CPI/PCE/PPI)
factors.summary: No new US inflation prints in news window. Next major catalyst is Friday NFP; PCE and CPI cycle not mentioned. Cannot assess fresh inflation surprise.

factors.name: Upcoming NFP & jobs data
factors.summary: May NFP report due Friday (tomorrow). Bloomberg Economics expects solid payrolls; Bessent (Treas Sec) commentary suggests job market strength. Solid jobs would support higher-for-longer Fed rates, headwind for gold, but data not yet released.

factors.name: Central bank gold buying
factors.summary: No new CB buying announcements in 24h window. Structural bid remains but not active catalyst.

screening.narrative: Gold is caught in a tug-of-war between de-escalating geopolitical risk (Iran peace talks, Israel-Lebanon ceasefire, Ukraine dialogue) and modest USD strength (DXY +27bps to 99.45) on hawkish Fed commentary (Schmid on inflation risk). Oil's 3% plunge on ceasefire optimism has eased inflation expectations, removing near-term inflation-hedge demand for gold. Price action remains choppy with intraday bounce off $4,424 lows, but lack of follow-through on 24h basis (-0.12%) and confined trading range signals indecision. Tomorrow's NFP and ongoing Treasury yield pressure are key near-term anchors.
catalysts_ahead: US May Nonfarm Payrolls (NFP) — Friday, June 5 (tomorrow AM EST) — solid jobs expected; could support higher-for-longer rates (gold headwind) or disappoint and trigger yield retreat (gold tailwind)
10-year Treasury yield trajectory — if yields climb ahead of NFP, bearish gold; if rates fade, bullish
Oil prices (WTI/Brent) — ceasefire narrative holding but subject to reversal; further drops eases inflation fears (bearish gold)
Fed speakers between now and next FOMC — any further hawkish commentary (like Schmid today) would support USD and cap gold
Trump-Iran/Ukraine diplomacy updates — any escalation reversal would further reduce geopolitical premium
screening.risk_warning: NFP surprise (either hot or cold) could swing bias sharply: strong jobs = higher real yields = bearish gold; weak jobs = flight-to-safety + yield drop = bullish gold. Geopolitical de-escalation narrative is dominant but fragile—any headlines of resumed conflict (Hezbollah rejection of ceasefire, new military clashes) could flip gold bullish immediately. Dollar strength (DXY near 99.45) is a headwind but not yet extreme; sustained break above 99.5 would cement bearish case.
screening.entry_guidance: Bias too choppy for high-conviction entry. If gold breaks above $4,510 on close, consider small long for NFP volatility hedge; if breaks below $4,424, test $4,400 support before adding shorts. Prefer to wait for NFP (24h away) to establish clearer direction rather than chase current range.
